options.content:
**Linear runbook for the auditor / on-call SRE — top to bottom**

1. **Stats row** — anything red = an attack path is live or recently was.
2. **OFFENDING POD** — every workload that attempted denied connections in the dashboard's time range, with its SPIFFE identity. Doesn't flap.
3. **OFFENDING DEPLOYMENT** — the malicious container running inside your cluster (image, age). This is the lateral attacker, not the mock-attacker (which is the receiving C2 stand-in).
4. **OFFENDING TOOL CALL** — the agentgateway audit trail for calls to the malicious tool, including which agent SPIFFE made the call.
5. **AuthZ deny log lines** — full forensic context per deny attempt.
6. **REMEDIATION** — kubectl commands to quarantine + roll back catalog.

panels.title: Art. 10 — AuthZ denies (live, last 5 min)
panels.description: Counts ztunnel-side denies in the last 5 minutes via Prometheus istio_tcp_connections_failed_total{response_flags=CONNECT}. Fixed 5-min window so reset-demo clears the panel within minutes - not whatever the dashboard's time range happens to be. Works in single- AND multi-cluster because metrics flow via the OTel pipeline.

panels.title: Exfil attempts: bank -> external-attacker (live, last 5 min)
panels.description: Counts TCP connection ATTEMPTS from any bank-side namespace into external-attacker in the last 5 minutes. Pre-policies-on this number = exfil that succeeded; post-policies-on this number = exfil that ztunnel blocked (cross-check with the AuthZ denies tile). Fixed 5-min window so reset clears the panel quickly.

panels.title: Art. 9(2) — % requests with mTLS (mesh-wide)
panels.description: Share of istio_requests_total where connection_security_policy=mutual_tls. Multi-cluster note: this label is emitted by ztunnel directly (not the gloo telemetry pipeline) so the value reflects the destination cluster Prometheus is scraping locally.

panels.title: Art. 17 — agent outbound calls (last 1h)
panels.description: Counts every outbound L7 request from the support-bot agent (A2A handoffs + MCP tool calls all go through here). The agentgateway waypoint itself doesn't appear as a destination in istio_requests_total because it's the waypoint, not a backend - so we use source-side metrics to capture the agent's activity volume.

panels.title: Art. 17 — kagent AccessPolicy denies (live, last 5 min)
panels.description: Counts agentgateway_requests_total{status=403, reason=Authorization} across all per-agent waypoints in the last 5 minutes. Each increment is a denied invocation: someone tried to call an Agent that an AccessPolicy doesn't allow them to invoke. Fires the KagentAccessPolicyDeny alert -> MailHog SOC inbox.

panels.title: Art. 17 — kagent AccessPolicy denials by waypoint (in time range)
panels.description: Per-agent breakdown of AccessPolicy 403s. Shows which target Agent the caller was trying to reach and how many times. Investigate by listing AccessPolicies in trustusbank-bank-agents and checking which subjects are allowed.

panels.title: 🚨 OFFENDING POD — pods that attempted denied connections (in time range)
panels.description: Persistent: queries the underlying counter, not live alert state. Stays populated across the dashboard's full time range so you don't lose evidence between attack bursts.

targets.expr: topk(20, sum by (source_workload, source_workload_namespace, source_principal, destination_service_namespace, destination_service_name) (increase(istio_tcp_connections_failed_total{response_flags="CONNECT"}[5m]))) > 0

panels.title: 📦 OFFENDING DEPLOYMENT — malicious container running INSIDE your cluster (image + age)
panels.description: kube-state-metrics view of the lateral-attacker workload — this is the rugpulled vendor MCP that's actually doing the exfil, not the mock-attacker (which only receives the data). Pull image, age, and pod name from here when remediating.

panels.title: 🔧 OFFENDING TOOL CALL — agentgateway audit trail (which agent invoked the malicious tool)
panels.description: Every tools/call request through agentgateway is logged here. Filter is the malicious vendor's name (acme-fx) plus the rugpulled tool (currency-converter). The src.addr at the start of each line maps back to a SPIFFE workload via the next panel down.

panels.title: Art. 10 — Istio AuthZ deny log lines (full forensic context per attempt)
Art. 10 — exfil attempts received by mock-attacker (Solo OFF state)
🛠️  REMEDIATION — what the on-call SRE should run

options.content:
Pull `source_workload` and `source_workload_namespace` from the OFFENDING POD table above, and walk the steps below.

```bash
# Defaults match this demo — substitute from the alert labels in production
SRC_NS=trustusbank-bank-vendors
SRC_WL=currency-converter

# 1. Confirm what's running — image tag, replicas, age
kubectl -n $SRC_NS describe deploy/$SRC_WL | grep -E 'Image:|Replicas:|Age:'

# 2. Quarantine — scale to 0 immediately, stops further attempts mid-incident
kubectl -n $SRC_NS scale deploy/$SRC_WL --replicas=0

# 3. Pull the catalog entry so CD can't re-deploy it
ARCTL_API_BASE_URL=http://localhost:18006 \
  arctl mcp delete acme-fx/currency-converter

# 4. Audit who called the bad tool — which agents invoked it, when
# (the OFFENDING TOOL CALL panel above shows this for the dashboard's time range)
kubectl -n trustusbank-platform logs deploy/trustusbank-agentgw \
  --since=1h | grep 'currency-converter'

# 5. If exfil succeeded (Solo was OFF when the attack ran), the
# mock-attacker.external-attacker logs hold the stolen records. Treat
# every record as breached and trigger DORA Art. 19 incident notification.
kubectl -n external-attacker logs deploy/mock-attacker --tail=500 \
  | grep stolen_data > /tmp/breach-records.json
```

**The chain that produced this dashboard view:**

  ztunnel rejects HBONE handshake → `istio_tcp_connections_failed_total{response_flags="CONNECT"}` increments → PodMonitor scrape → `IstioAuthZDeny` PrometheusRule fires → Alertmanager + the table at the top of this dashboard light up. Independently, ztunnel emits a single `error access connection complete … explicitly denied by …` log line that Promtail ships into Loki — that's the deny-log panel.

panels.title: Art. 9 — agentgateway access log (every MCP request audited, all tools)
Art. 9(2) — ztunnel: SPIFFE identity per HBONE connection
